automatic_gate_requires: immutable content fingerprint and frozen validation contract
no deterministic fail, warning, indeterminate or error
two independent decision-grade DeepSeek solutions agree
subject critic passes self-containment, reference consistency, chemistry, units and applicability
rubric and final answer are structurally consistent

contracts.core_invariant: c1*V1 equals c2*V2 for one conserved analyte and one dilution stage.

types.c1: amount or mass concentration measurement with unit

contracts.coverage_boundary: No reaction, density correction or chained aliquot may be hidden inside this typed core; more complex sample preparation requires a different certified blueprint.

contracts.core_invariant: Analyte and titrant extents at equivalence agree under one explicitly named factor convention.
contracts.coverage_boundary: The check verifies equivalence stoichiometry, not indicator choice, activity corrections, endpoint bias or a laboratory procedure. These require explicit data and semantic review.

contracts.core_invariant: Fg=(nu_analyte*M_analyte)/(nu_weighing_form*M_weighing_form) and m_analyte=Fg*m_weighing_form agree with one explicit stoichiometric relation.

types.analyte_molar_mass: positive molar-mass measurement with unit, stated numerically in the task
types.weighing_form_molar_mass: positive molar-mass measurement with unit, stated numerically in the task
types.weighing_form_mass: positive mass measurement with unit, stated numerically in the task

contracts.coverage_boundary: The check validates the factor and analyte-mass chain only. Identity and purity of the weighing form, completeness of precipitation/ignition, sample mass, mass fraction and uncertainty still require explicit task data and independent solver/critic review.

contracts.core_invariant: G=1/R and kappa=K_cell*G agree after converting resistance, conductance, inverse-length and conductivity units.

types.resistance: positive resistance measurement with unit; either R or matching G is stated numerically in the task
types.conductance: positive conductance measurement with unit; either G or matching R is stated numerically in the task
types.cell_constant: positive inverse-length measurement with unit, stated numerically in the task

contracts.coverage_boundary: The check validates one certified cell constant and the R/G/kappa chain. It does not derive K_cell from a reference solution, verify temperature compensation, electrode polarization, concentration dependence or molar conductivity.

contracts.core_invariant: Q=I*t, n_e=Q/F and m=M*Q*eta/(zF) agree, and the numeric F used by the solution is explicitly visible in the task statement.

types.faraday_constant: positive charge-per-amount measurement with C/mol unit, stated numerically in the task

contracts.coverage_boundary: The module accepts no hidden Faraday constant: F=96485.33212 C/mol must be explicit in the statement and structured facts. The electrochemical identity of the reaction and z still require an explicit equation and agent criticism.

contracts.core_invariant: The final Q and n_e/mass relations agree after the profile has been integrated, using the numeric F explicitly visible in the task statement.

types.faraday_constant: positive charge-per-amount measurement with C/mol unit, stated numerically in the task

contracts.coverage_boundary: F=96485.33212 C/mol must be explicit in the statement and structured facts. The current validator does not recompute a piecewise integral from an interval array. Independent solvers and the critic must recompute every interval; the deterministic core checks only downstream Faraday relations. A task may not claim deterministic coverage of the integration itself.

contracts.core_invariant: For y=a*c+b, c=(signal-intercept)/slope and c lies inside the certified range.

types.concentration: finite number in the same numeric scale as calibration_range

contracts.coverage_boundary: The check verifies inversion and flags extrapolation. It does not independently fit raw calibration points, validate weighting, blank correction, heteroscedasticity or detection limits.

semantic_only_blueprints.reason: No deterministic engine decides selectivity or matrix interference.
No SPM calibration/artifact deterministic engine is implemented.
